# AI Exposure of the European Job Market

## Project
An interactive treemap visualizing AI exposure across ~130 European occupation groups (ISCO-08 3-digit level). Built by Philipp Maul / Nexalps.

## Methodology
- Occupation data from ESCO v1.2.1 (~3,000 occupations with rich descriptions)
- Employment statistics from Eurostat EU-LFS 2024 (ISCO-08 2-digit, distributed to 3-digit using ESCO structural weights)
- Wage data from Eurostat Structure of Earnings Survey 2022
- AI exposure scoring via Claude Sonnet 4 (Anthropic), producing two scores per group:
  - technical_score (0-10): Pure AI capability — how much CAN AI reshape this job?
  - regulated_score (0-10): Practical European exposure, factoring in EU AI Act high-risk obligations, works council consultation, employment protection, GDPR constraints — how much WILL AI reshape this job in the EU?
- Aggregated to ISCO-08 3-digit level (~130 groups) for treemap visualization

## Key Findings
- 199.6M total jobs covered (EU-27)
- Average technical exposure: 5.6 / 10
- Average regulated exposure: 4.4 / 10
- Average regulatory delta: 1.2 points (regulation reduces practical AI exposure)
- Exposure-weighted wages: ~€4.7T (technical) vs ~€3.7T (regulated)
- Largest deltas in HR, legal, education, and public administration occupations where EU AI Act Annex III + works council requirements create significant friction

## Data Sources
- ESCO v1.2.1 (European Commission): https://esco.ec.europa.eu
- Eurostat EU-LFS 2024: https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat
- Eurostat SES 2022 (wages): https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat
- ISCO-08 (ILO): International Standard Classification of Occupations

## Attribution
Inspired by Andrej Karpathy's AI Exposure Map (https://karpathy.ai/jobs/).
Extended with dual-score methodology for the European regulatory context.

- Code: MIT (Philipp Maul / Nexalps)

- Built on ESCO (EU Commission) and Eurostat data
